text:
Establish and ENFORCE a repo-local way of working: every change to THIS repository must go through a lume workstream. The enforcement targets the agent (Claude's tool calls), not the human operator's own direct edits, and it is a property of THIS repo only - it must NOT become a lume feature that ships to lume's users.

WHY. lume's whole premise is operator-gated, traceable work. If the agent can edit and land changes outside any workstream, the repo's own history stops reflecting that discipline. A lightweight, repo-scoped guardrail keeps the agent honest without changing lume's behaviour for anyone else.

MECHANISM (decided). A project-scoped Claude Code PreToolUse hook in .claude/settings.json (committed, so it applies to anyone driving this repo with the agent) that DENIES `git commit` unless an active lume workstream has a current iteration in the `working` phase. Chosen scope: COMMIT-ONLY chokepoint - the agent may read, explore, and edit freely, but nothing LANDS (is committed) outside a workstream's working phase. The hook fires on the agent's tool calls; edits the human makes directly in their own editor are not policed (hence 'more for the agent than the operator'). A repo-root CLAUDE.md states the rule for the agent in prose. Nothing is added under plugin/ - the rule does not ship to lume users.

DONE-WHEN. (a) A PreToolUse hook (script + .claude/settings.json wiring) denies `git commit` when no active workstream has a working-phase iteration, and allows it when one does, with a clear deny reason shown to the agent. (b) The hook is committed in repo-scoped config (.claude/), NOT under plugin/, and changes no lume engine code. (c) A repo-root CLAUDE.md documents the way of working (all changes via a workstream; how to start one; the commit gate) as agent-facing guidance. (d) The carve-outs are correct: commits are blocked only by absence of a working iteration; the human's direct (non-agent) edits are unaffected; files outside this repo are unaffected. (e) The hook is verified to deny and allow as specified (a dry-run of the decision logic on both states). (f) This very workstream's own changes are committed through it, dogfooding the rule.

NOT. Not a lume engine change. Not shipped in the plugin. Not an adversarial sandbox (an agent could still mangle files via raw Bash redirects - this is honest guidance enforced at the commit chokepoint, not a security boundary). Not a constraint on the operator's manual edits.
title: Enforce: all repo changes go through a lume workstream (repo-only commit gate)
